violations.text: Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026
Grapes were not washed before being put on toothpicks. Plant foods must be thoroughly washed in water to remove soil and other contaminants before they are cut. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026
Dipper well was not turned on, leaving the ice cream scoops in stagnant water. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026
The 3 bay sink was not in good repair, with sealant pulling away from the surface, and is not smooth and easily cleanable. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2026
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 04/06/2026
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/26/2023

Working containers of ice cream toppings are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/26/2023
